pages.text: One morning, <name> found a shiny pair of chappals in the garden. Curious, he slipped them on. The moment he took a step, whoosh! — he leaped over the fence into the neighbor’s yard!

“Whoa!” he shouted, landing softly on the grass. The neighbor’s cat yowled and darted away, and <name> giggled, wobbling to keep balance. He jumped again… and splash! He landed in the pond, sending water flying everywhere. Ducks quacked in surprise, and <name> laughed so hard he almost slipped.

By now, he realized the chappals were magical. Every step sent him flying farther than expected. He tried running carefully, but instead of walking to school, he zoomed across the playground, spinning mid-air like a superhero. His friends stared in awe, mouths wide open.

<name> experimented, learning to control the jumps. A small hop now landed him safely on the grass. A medium leap could reach the top of the slide. With careful steps, he discovered he could land exactly where he wanted — even doing tiny tricks for fun.

Soon, he invited his friends to try the chappals (one at a time!). They laughed and jumped over puddles, flower beds, and little fences, cheering as each landing was safe and exciting. The chappals made everyone giggle as they soared and spun, but no more unexpected pond splashes!

By the end of the day, <name> sat under a tree, tapping the chappals together. He felt proud, having learned to control the magic, and happy sharing the fun with friends. Every jump was now safe, thrilling, and filled with laughter, making the afternoon a magical adventure he would never forget.
